description: Widening or disabling the watchdog to stop spurious resets, removing the safety net that would recover a hung device in the field.
body: Failure mode: the watchdog keeps resetting the device, so the period is extended or the watchdog disabled to 'fix' it; the underlying hang (deadlock, ISR storm, infinite loop) remains and now the device can lock up permanently in the field with no recovery. Detection signals: reset-cause register shows watchdog resets before the change; resets stop but occasional permanent hangs begin; the kick path contains a blocking or unbounded operation. Do not disable or lengthen the watchdog to mask a hang. Safer fallback: capture the reset cause and the blocked kick path, fix the root-cause hang, keep the watchdog at a tight period, and verify recovery by injecting the failure.
